jobs.title: Assistant Program Director (West 140th)
jobs.description:
Organization Overview:
The Goddard Riverside Community Center (Goddard Riverside) and the Stanley M. Isaacs Neighborhood Center (Isaacs Center) are well-established Manhattan community-based agencies that support services to approximately 30,000 New Yorkers. We are two of New York City’s leading human services organizations meeting the needs of children, youth and families, homeless individuals, and older adults through-out Manhattan and New York City. We work every day for a fair and just society where all people have the opportunity to make choices that lead to better lives for themselves and their families.

Program Description:
Goddard Riverside’s 140 West 140th Residence, located in West Harlem, provides affordable, permanent supportive housing for 48 previously homeless adults suffering from mental illness and/or chemical addictions. The building consists of 48 fully furnished studio apartments, offices for the social service and building staff, a recreation room/tenant lounge, kitchen, library and backyard. On-site services include case management, entitlement assistance, medical care, work readiness training, meals programs, 24-hour/7 –day front desk security, on site group activities, substance use counseling and medical and therapeutic care.

Position Summary:
West 140th Street is looking for a dynamic individual who will work with the Program Director to ensure the quality and integrity of the program. This person must be innovative, creative, detailed oriented, strengths based, with organizational skills that will promote a proactive, high energy work ethic and positive work environment. The Assistant Program Director is responsible for integrating services, staff supervision, and assuming leadership of West 140th in the absence on the Program Director.

Schedule:
Monday through Friday 9:00 am too 5:00 pm

Salary Range:
$70,000-$75,000

Candidates must have the required qualifications to be considered for the position. The s alary offer will be based on the candidate’s number of years experience in the exact or comparable position and additional preferred qualifications will be taken into account.

Show less

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Other

Industries

Non-profit Organizations

jobs.title: Program Director
jobs.description:
Requirements

The ideal candidate will possess:

Master's Degree in psychology, counseling, or another health and human service-related field required.
Licensed in PA or license eligible as a Marriage and Family Therapist (LMFT), Social Worker (LSW), Clinical Social Worker (LCSW), Professional Counselor (LPC), or Behavior Specialist (LBS)
Experience in the behavioral health field with:
Two years in a mental health setting.
Two years working with individuals with developmental disabilities.

Possess at least two years of program management experience.

Are you looking for an opportunity to advance your career while working with an extraordinary team? At Merakey, we put heart and soul into everything we do. We are seeking a Program Director
to join our team in our Wilkes Barre, PA
location.

Starting salary of $62,000 annually, or salary aligned with experience and licensure, plus mileage reimbursement.

Position Details

Developing and demonstrating a collaborative work environment in support of Merakey’s Core Values.
Creating a culture that fully engages employees and empowers others to suggest and make decisions for continuing improvement.
Providing ongoing guidance, mentoring, feedback, and motivation to staff to ensure competent performance while adhering to department and Merakey policies and procedures.
Ensuring compliance to state, local, federal, and contract requirements, as well as requirements from payors.
Overseeing the profit and loss for the program and managing daily operations.
Maintaining productive working relationships and communication with external and internal stakeholders.
Overseeing recruitment and new hire onboarding processes.

What is a Dual Diagnosis Treatment Team?

Merakey’s Dual Diagnosis Treatment Team (DDTT) is a recovery-oriented program supporting individuals with both mental illness and intellectual disabilities. Through a high-touch, community-based model, DDTT delivers integrated mental health treatment, service coordination, and rehabilitation—empowering individuals to thrive in their homes and communities. As part of a close-knit, multidisciplinary team, you’ll gain hands-on experience, 24/7 support, and the opportunity to make a real difference—making DDTT an ideal choice for those seeking meaningful, mission-driven work with room to grow. DDTT strengthens communities by reducing hospitalizations and promoting independence and inclusion and we'd love for you to be part of it.

Click here to learn more about Merakey's DDTT programs.

Benefits

Merakey offers generous benefits that promote well-being, financial security, and work-life balance, including:

Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage, plus access to healthcare advocacy support.
Retirement plan -- both pre-tax and Roth (after-tax) options available for employee contributions.
DailyPay -- access your pay when you need it!
On the Goga well-being platform, featuring self-care tools and resources.
Access Care.com for backup childcare, elder care, and household services.
Confidential counseling, legal, and financial services through our Employee Assistance Program (EAP).
Tuition reimbursement and educational partnerships.
Employee discounts and savings programs on entertainment, travel, and lifestyle.
Access to Pryor Online Learning for free online personal development classes.

Learn more about our full benefits package - https://www.merakey.org/careers/benefits

About Merakey

Merakey is a non-profit provider of developmental, behavioral health, and education services. More than 8,000 employees provide support to nearly 40,000 individuals and families throughout 12 states across the country each year. Click here to watch a video about Merakey. Merakey strictly follows a zero-tolerance policy for abuse.

Merakey is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer!
We deeply value diversity and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, ethnic background, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, genetic information, or disability status. Moreover, we are committed to creating teams that reflect the diversity of the communities we serve and encourage applicants from underrepresented backgrounds to apply. Merakey welcomes all Veterans to apply!

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Not Applicable

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Business Development and Sales

Industries

Non-profit Organizations, Hospitals, and Mental Health Care

Must hold a Master’s or Doctorate degree in Social Work, Psychology, or a related Human Services field.

jobs.company: The University of Texas at San Antonio
jobs.title: Director of Communications - University Strategic Communications
jobs.description:
Position Information

The University of Texas at San Antonio
(UT San Antonio) is a nationally recognized, top-tier public research university that unites the power of higher education, biomedical discovery and healthcare within one visionary institution. As the third-largest research university in Texas and a Carnegie R1-designated institution, UT San Antonio is a model of access and excellence — advancing knowledge, social mobility and public health across South Texas and beyond. UT San Antonio serves approximately 42,000 students in 320 academic programs spanning science, engineering, medicine, health, liberal arts, AI, cybersecurity, business, education and more. With 17,000 faculty and staff, UT San Antonio has also been recognized as a Top Employer in Texas by Forbes Magazine. Learn more online , on UT San Antonio Today or on X , Instagram , Facebook , YouTube or LinkedIn .

Salary Range
: $87,500 - $96,900/Annualized, commensurate with education, experience, and qualifications.

Job Type
: Full Time

Posting Close Date
: Applications will be accepted through 11:59 PM CDT on 7/17/2026. At the discretion of the hiring department, this posting may close once a sufficient number of qualified applications have been received.

Required Application Materials
:

Resume is required.
Cover letter is required.
Contact information for three professional references.

Job Details

Job Summary

The Director of Communications oversees the planning, development and execution of comprehensive communication programs that support the institution’s strategic priorities. Manages multiple communication initiatives, coordinates cross-functional teams, and ensures that work products are delivered on time, on brand, and aligned with institutional goals. Collaborates with colleagues to maintain a high standard of quality, messaging consistency, and relevance across communications channels.

Core Responsibilities

Writes and edits various communications, including institutional reputation articles, thought leadership documents, and collateral materials to promote the institution, its leadership, its schools, and units.
Develops and manages the execution of large and small-scale communications plans from initial concept through completion.
Serves as lead editor to ensure the quality and continuity of messaging across communications platforms, including publications, web communications, media relations, social media, internal communications, and marketing collateral.
Collaborates with media relations, social media, internal communications, multimedia/digital, and marketing professionals to design, develop, and execute highly impactful storytelling that fosters a positive impression of the university.
Regularly reviews and maintains strategic messaging materials across the institution are regularly reviewed to ensure accuracy and brand standards.
Maintains budgets and timelines related to specific projects.
Oversees and evaluates analytics and impact of communications programs;
Serves as a liaison to communications colleagues from other colleges, divisions, and units.
Performs other duties as assigned.

Required Qualifications

Bachelor's Degree in a related field.
Seven (7) years of related work experience.
This position requires the ability to maintain the security and integrity of UT San Antonio and its infrastructure.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities

Independent Decision Making
Problem Solving
Verbal & Written Communication
Leadership
Creativity
Digital Transformation
Strategic Planning
Collaboration
Creativity
Strategic Communications, Public Relations, Branding, Multimedia
Analytical and Critical Thinking
Editing Skills

Working Conditions

Office environment. Ability to travel to and from meetings, training sessions, or other business-related events. Workdays, areas, and work hours may vary depending on departmental needs.

Physical Demands

Sedentary: Up to 10lbs - Ability to exert up to 10 pounds of force to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects.

This position will work both on campus and remotely in Texas
. Travel and parking expenses are the employee’s responsibility.

This position is contingent upon a successful background check. Verification of a valid driver’s license and Motor Vehicle Record (MVR) may be completed as applicable. Applicants selected must be able to show proof of eligibility to work in the United States by time of hire.

Equal Employment Opportunity

As an equal employment opportunity and affirmative action employer, it is the policy of The University of Texas at San Antonio to promote and ensure equal employment opportunity for all individuals regardless of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability or genetic information, and veteran status. The University is committed to the Affirmative Action Program in compliance with all government requirements to ensure nondiscrimination.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Director

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Marketing, Public Relations, and Writing/Editing

Industries

Higher Education

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer: (Construction)
jobs.description:
Seeking senior executives to join the firm. The role is currently a non-Executive role that would be transitioned to a full-time executive role soon.

Looking for motivated professionals with 20+ experience in Smart City Project operations.

Duties & Responsibilities

In the executive capacity: Overseeing the day-to-day administrative and operational functions of a business.
In the non-executive capacity, Analyse potential acquisition target from an operations point of view
Deliver results on a day-to-day, quarter-to-quarter basis
Report to the CEO

Required Knowledge & Skills

Strong analytical, managerial, communication, and leadership skills.
Highly skilled and experienced in Construction operations.
